name: Somesh Rao's Snacks: Bhelpuri
recipeIngredient: 1 package Bhel mix or Sev (thin deep-fried chick Pea-flour noodles)
3 tbsp Freshly roasted and ground cumin
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the potato mixture: Boil potatoes, mash them, salt to taste, add pepper.
2. Chop fresh coriander leaves.
3. Roast cumin seeds and grind into powder.
4. Make tamarind sauce: Dissolve 4 tbsp tamarind concentrate in 1 cup hot water, simmer until thickened. Add jaggery or sugar to achieve a tart-sweet balance; add salt and ground red paprika if desired. Sauce should be thinner than maple syrup.
5. Combine puffed rice and Bhel mix in a large bowl.
6. On serving plates, layer the dish: start with the rice-Bhel mixture, add mashed potatoes, then onions, chilies. Sprinkle cumin powder.
7. Drizzle tamarind sauce over the top and garnish with fresh coriander leaves.
8. Season with additional salt or pepper if needed.
This recipe is inspired by traditional Bhelpuri from Indian street vendors. Quantities are approximate; adjust to taste. For purists, visit an Indian grocery for authentic ingredients.
